content:
Hello there and welcome! I am currently pursuing a Ph.D. degree in Statistics at the Department of Statistical Sciences, University of Padova, Italy. My doctoral research, under the fully enthusiastic and dedicated guidance of Professor Alberto Roverato, mainly focuses on the problems concerning model selection for graphical models for the large biological data. The full description for those who are interested is available in my attached curriculum vitae.
My research interests typically surround statistical learning and applied statistics, in particular, I would apply methodologies in machine learning together with statistical modelling to analyze and interpret the large and complex data sets, especially data relating to biology and genetics.
I am a young and motivated researcher and I am on the path of building the scientific world of my own. It will definitely be an honor for me to be in any collaborative relationships.
summary: Hello there and welcome! I am currently pursuing a Ph.D. degree in Statistics at the Department of Statistical Sciences, University of Padova, Italy. My doctoral research, under the fully enthusiastic and dedicated guidance of Professor Alberto Roverato, mainly focuses on the problems concerning model selection for graphical models for the large biological data.
title: Dung Ngoc Nguyen

summary: The activity is part of the AFOSR project and as a specific object the identification of a network, based on a set of data in the event that the reference context presents a natural symmetrical structure. Specifically, the network can be seen as the union of two blocks, each vertex in the first block has a homologous vertex within the second block, and two blocks are interconnected. This problem can be seen as a special case of a colored graphical model in which the model space forms a lattice structure. The problem of model selection through greedy procedures therefore requires the development of algorithms that allow the search space to be efficiently explored through local steps.
tags: Algorithms for graphical models with symmetries
title: Algorithms for graphical models with symmetries
